title: Dreamy Pillowy Ethiopian Lentils Shiro
description: A pungent and pillowy Ethiopian dish featuring peppercorns and kombu-cold-extract, perfect for a post-workout meal, this shiro is a shojin-friendly form of traditional Ethiopian cuisine.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and saute until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and saute for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the whole black peppercorns and saute for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the rinsed and drained red lentils, kombu-cold-extract, berbere spice, and salt, and stir to combine.
Add the water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and have broken apart.
Use an immersion blender to puree the lentils to a pillowy consistency, or allow them to cool and puree them in a blender.
Serve the shiro hot, garnished with additional berbere spice if desired.
